summary: The presence of coarse tremors typically indicates disorders such as Parkinson’s disease or advanced stages of neurosyphilitic manifestations; these are not associated with hyperthyroidism which usually presents more subtle hand tremors at rest rather than coarse ones involving speech organs specifically.
